picks.rationale: The Under has been a strong trend in recent matchups between the Knicks and Hawks, hitting in four of their last five head-to-head games. Both games in the current playoff series have also gone Under the total, indicating a defensive struggle. The Knicks boast a top-tier defensive rating, and with Atlanta's Jock Landale out due to injury, offensive efficiency may be further hampered. Given the playoff intensity and historical scoring patterns, another lower-scoring contest is highly probable.

The Cleveland Cavaliers enter Game 3 with a commanding 2-0 series lead and have won their last five games overall, demonstrating strong momentum. Toronto is facing significant injury concerns with Immanuel Quickley (hamstring) and Ja'Kobe Walter (illness) both listed as questionable, and Quickley did not play in Game 2. The Cavaliers have a solid 25-16 road record against the spread this season, indicating their ability to perform well away from home. With Donovan Mitchell and James Harden leading a potent offense, Cleveland is well-positioned to cover the spread.

The first two games of this playoff series between the Nuggets and Timberwolves have both gone Over the total, suggesting a high-scoring affair. Denver ranks first in the league in scoring, averaging 122.1 points per game, and has hit the Over in 80% of their last five games. Despite the high line, both teams have shown offensive firepower, with Minnesota winning Game 2 with 119 points. With Aaron Gordon probable for Denver and Anthony Edwards playing well for Minnesota despite being questionable, expect another offensive showcase.

The New York Knicks, with a superior 53-29 regular-season record, hold a 3-2 advantage in their last five head-to-head contests against the Hawks, including two wins in this playoff series. Despite dropping Game 2, the Knicks possess a strong overall defensive identity and will be motivated to regain control of the series on the road. Atlanta is without Jock Landale, which could impact their depth. The Knicks' overall strength and playoff experience give them a decisive edge to secure a road victory.

While the Cavaliers hold a 2-0 series lead, the Raptors are returning home for a crucial Game 3, facing a must-win situation to avoid an insurmountable deficit. Toronto has a respectable 21-20 record against the spread in home games this season, while Cleveland has struggled on the road ATS with a 17-24 record. Although Immanuel Quickley and Ja'Kobe Walter are questionable, the desperation of a home playoff game often fuels strong performances. The Raptors' determination to stay in the series, combined with their home-court advantage, makes them a strong play to cover the spread.

The Carolina Hurricanes enter Game 3 with a commanding 2-0 series lead over the Ottawa Senators, having won Game 1 (2-0) and Game 2 (3-2 OT). Carolina has also dominated the recent head-to-head matchups, winning four of the last five regular season and playoff encounters. Despite Ottawa playing at home and the return of defenseman Tyler Kleven, the Hurricanes' superior record (53-22-7 vs. 44-27-11) and strong playoff performance make them a solid pick to continue their winning ways. The Hurricanes are a well-structured team that has consistently found ways to win against the Senators.

The Colorado Avalanche are a formidable team with a 55-16-11 record and have been dominant against the Los Angeles Kings, winning their last five head-to-head matchups. Colorado already holds a 1-0 series lead after a decisive 2-1 victory in Game 1. The Kings, in contrast, have struggled recently, with four losses in their last five games. Given the Avalanche's strong form, their historical dominance over the Kings, and their early series lead, they are well-positioned to cover the -1.5 spread on the road.

The head-to-head history between the Buffalo Sabres and Boston Bruins suggests a high-scoring affair, with three of their last five matchups, including two playoff games, going over 6.5 goals. Both teams have shown offensive capabilities recently; the Sabres boast a +8 goal differential in their last 10 games, and the Bruins won Game 2 of this series by a score of 4-2. With the series tied 1-1, both teams will be pushing hard for offense in this crucial Game 3, likely leading to an open and high-scoring contest.

Despite the Hurricanes' overall offensive prowess, the recent trend in this specific playoff series points to lower-scoring games. Game 1 was a 2-0 shutout, and Game 2 was a tight 3-2 overtime affair, indicating strong defensive play from both sides. Furthermore, the Carolina Hurricanes have gone under their projected goal total in their last four games, and the Ottawa Senators have done so in four of their last five. The line movement from an initial O/U 6 to 5.5, with the Under at favorable odds, also suggests that sharp money is backing a lower-scoring outcome.

Logan Stankoven has been an offensive force for the Carolina Hurricanes, scoring a goal in each of the first two games of this series against the Ottawa Senators. His recent form is exceptional, as he has registered a point in each of his last 10 games, accumulating nine goals and five assists in that span. Stankoven is active and expected to play for the Hurricanes today. Given his consistent scoring and hot streak, he is a strong candidate to find the back of the net once again in Game 3.

The Chicago Cubs are on an impressive eight-game winning streak and have already swept the first three games of this series against the struggling Phillies, who have lost eight straight. While Phillies starter Cristopher Sanchez has a strong 1.59 ERA, Cubs starter Edward Cabrera has a 2.38 ERA, and the Cubs' overall team offense and pitching staff are performing better. The line movement, with the Cubs' moneyline tightening from +109 to +104 despite opening as underdogs, indicates sharper money backing Chicago. The Cubs are at home and have a significant momentum advantage, making them a strong play at plus money.

picks.game: San Diego Padres vs Colorado Rockies
picks.rationale: This game is at Coors Field, which is known for high-scoring affairs. Both starting pitchers, Matt Waldron (14.73 ERA) for the Padres and Ryan Feltner (6.00 ERA) for the Rockies, have struggled significantly this season. Waldron allowed six runs in his first start, and Feltner gave up six runs, seven hits, and three homers to the Padres earlier this month. The Padres' lineup has a .359 career batting average and .954 OPS against Feltner, suggesting they can exploit this matchup. With two struggling pitchers in a hitter-friendly park, the Over 11 is highly probable.

picks.game: Chicago White Sox vs Arizona Diamondbacks
picks.rationale: The Arizona Diamondbacks hold a clear advantage over the Chicago White Sox in team offense and overall profile. Arizona won the previous game in the series 11-7, and they have a better record in day games (6-3 vs. 5-8). While White Sox starter Davis Martin has a respectable 2.16 ERA, Diamondbacks starter Mike Soroka is also performing well with a 4-0 record and 2.78 ERA. The line movement shows sharper money backing Arizona, with their moneyline strengthening from -142 to -156.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers vs San Francisco Giants
picks.rationale: Despite losing the first two games of this series, the Los Angeles Dodgers possess a superior overall team profile and pitching advantage with Tyler Glasnow on the mound. Glasnow boasts an excellent 2.62 xERA and 24.2 K-BB%, while Giants starter Logan Webb has struggled with a 4.42 xERA and high hard-contact rate. The Dodgers' offense, despite being limited in the series so far, is a top-tier unit in every category. This is a bounce-back spot for a strong Dodgers team looking to avoid a sweep against a Giants team with significant injuries.

picks.game: New York Yankees vs Boston Red Sox
picks.rationale: The New York Yankees are on a five-game winning streak and have already secured a 2-0 series lead against the Red Sox with dominant 4-0 and 4-1 victories. Yankees starter Cam Schlittler has been exceptional with a 1.95 ERA and 0.76 WHIP, while the Red Sox are starting Payton Tolle, who is considered outmatched. The Yankees also hold a significant power advantage, with 34 home runs compared to Boston's 13. Overwhelming public support and line movement towards the Yankees further reinforce confidence in them covering the run line.
